channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: INEC Denies Fake 𝕏 Account Linked to Chairman Praising APC Win
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) don talk say dem no get any hand inside one social media wahala wey dey spread online. Some people bin circulate screenshots from an 𝕏 account wey...

item.title: Woman on trial for murder reportedly confessed plans to kill her sister, court told
item.description: According to court proceedings in the United Kingdom, a woman accused of murdering her sister in what has become known as the 'Rolex murder case' allegedly confided in her sibling that she was plannin...

item.title: Understanding El Niño: Why 'Super' Is a Misleading Term
item.description: Australian climate scientists are expressing frustration with the media's use of sensationalist terms like 'Super El Niño' or 'Godzilla El Niño', as these phrases create unnecessary anxiety among farm...

item.title: TUC urges Government to use excess crude revenue to reduce petrol price
item.description: The Trade Union Congress (TUC) don raise serious concern say petrol price fit soon reach ₦2,000 per litre if Federal Government no take quick action. During press conference wey happen for Abuja, the ...

item.title: Bushbuckridge principal dismissed after investigation confirms misconduct with pupil
item.description: A principal from Magwagwaza Secondary School in Bushbuckridge, Mpumalanga, has been dismissed after an Education Labour Relations Council (ELRC) inquiry found him guilty of having a sexual relationshi...

item.title: Iran's Digital Warfare: Targeting Tech Infrastructure and Spreading Disinformation
item.description: A recent report has highlighted Iran's increasingly sophisticated approach to digital warfare, specifically targeting technology companies and leveraging disinformation campaigns. The report details h...

item.title: NASA’s Artemis II crew safely returns to Earth after record-breaking lunar flyby
item.description: NASA’s Artemis II mission has successfully concluded with its four astronauts returning safely to Earth after completing a historic lunar flyby—the first crewed Moon venture in more than half a centur...

item.title: Artemis II Crew Safely Returns to Earth After Lunar Flyby, Setting Distance Record
item.description: The Artemis II mission, a landmark moment for the U.S. space program, successfully concluded on Friday (April 10, 2026) with the crew capsule splashing down off the California coast. The four astronau...

item.title: Tinubu Urges Nigerians To Remain Hopeful Despite Rising Fuel Prices
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu don talk say Nigerians suppose get hope despite the hardship wey dey follow fuel price increase. During him visit to Bayelsa State where him commission some projects, Tinub...

item.title: CSU Channel Islands Professor Cleared in Federal Assault Case Over 2025 Immigration Protest
item.description: A federal jury in Los Angeles acquitted Jonathan Caravello, a 38-year-old math and philosophy professor at California State University Channel Islands, of assaulting federal agents with a dangerous we...

item.title: UK Government Reconsiders Online Porn Restrictions to Ban Step-Relative and 'Barely Legal' Content
item.description: The UK government has announced plans to ban pornographic material depicting sexual activity between step-relatives and 'barely legal' performers as part of its broader regulation of online adult cont...

item.title: 12-year-old boy dies in swimming pool hours after baptism preparations in Kirinyaga
item.description: A tragic incident unfolded in Ngurubani, Mwea, Kirinyaga County, when a 12-year-old boy drowned in a pool just hours after attending baptism preparations at St. Joseph Allamano Wanguru Catholic Church...

item.title: Geordin Hill-Lewis and Sibusiso Dyonase Contend for DA Leadership at Federal Congress
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) is holding its largest federal congress yet at the Gallagher Convention Centre in Midrand, Johannesburg, to elect a new party leader following John Steenhuisen’s decision ...

item.title: Migrant Workers from Cooch Behar Disrupt Jaipur's Daily Life as They Head Back Home to Vote
item.description: Ahead of Bengal's crucial April 23 polls, a significant number of migrant workers from Cooch Behar have taken leave from their jobs in Jaipur to exercise their right to vote. With more than 80% of dom...

item.title: Tharaka Nithi Woman Shares the Qualities She Wants in a Husband
item.description: A 32-year-old woman from Tharaka Nithi, Rosemary Kagendo, has openly sought help to find a husband, stating that she wants someone she can stay with through joy and hardship. She outlined the key qual...

item.title: Kenya fuel import scandal exposes deep corruption and institutional failure
item.description: This opinion piece by Lewis Ngunyi sharply criticises the recent fuel scandal in Kenya, where emergency imports of fuel outside the normal Government-to-Government arrangements led to serious allegati...

item.title: BINI Makes History with Landmark Coachella Performance
item.description: BINI, the Filipino pop group, made history with their first-ever performance at the internationally renowned Coachella music festival. Their debut was widely celebrated as a milestone for P-Pop, showc...

item.title: Global aviation sector faces fuel supply risk due to ongoing U.S.–Iran tensions and closure of the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The Airports Council International (ACI), a global organization representing airports worldwide, has raised serious concerns about potential jet fuel shortages if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed d...

item.title: Wole Soyinka and the Silence of the Sage in Tinubu’s Nigeria
item.description: At almost ninety-two years old, Wole Soyinka still dey strong and wise, but many Nigerians dey wonder why the old lion dey keep quiet while President Tinubu’s government dey commit many wahala. The wr...

item.title: Montreal Gynecologist Suspended for Inappropriate and Humiliating Medical Practices
item.description: A Montreal gynecologist has been suspended for three months after performing what has been described as ‘traumatic’ and ‘humiliating’ medical procedures on patients. The suspension follows a thorough ...
